name: SigDigger
description: Qt-based digital signal analyzer, using Suscan core and Sigutils DSP library
readme_summary: SigDigger is a free, Qt-based digital signal analyzer for GNU/Linux and macOS that extracts information from unknown radio signals. It supports various SDR devices via SoapySDR and offers adjustable demodulation for FSK, PSK, and ASK signals, along with analog video decoding and voice channel listening.
key_features: Digital signal analysis
Support for various SDR devices via SoapySDR
Adjustable demodulation of FSK, PSK, and ASK signals
Analog video decoding
Analysis of bursty signals
Listening to analog voice channels
Real-time processing
Qt-based GUI
primary_use_case: Analyzing and extracting information from unknown radio signals using SDR hardware.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled radio operator, depicted as a modern-day alchemist, stands in a dimly lit workshop filled with vintage and futuristic radio equipment. They are holding a glowing, ethereal radio wave, carefully dissecting it with a digital magnifying glass that reveals intricate patterns and symbols within the wave. The input radio wave is a chaotic swirl of static and faint signals, while the output is a clear, organized stream of decoded data, represented by glowing geometric shapes and text. Subtle UI elements from SigDigger are projected onto a nearby workbench, showing signal parameters and decoded information. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, with a focus on dramatic lighting and a color palette of deep blues, purples, and glowing oranges, emphasizing the transformation of raw signals into understandable information.

name: Look4Sat
description: Satellite tracker and pass predictor for Android, inspired by Gpredict
readme_summary: Look4Sat is an Android application that tracks satellites and predicts their passes. Inspired by Gpredict, it uses data from Celestrak and SatNOGS to provide real-time positional data, pass predictions, and ground track visualizations for over 9000 satellites.
key_features: Predicting satellite positions and passes for up to 10 days
Showing the list of currently active and upcoming satellite passes
Showing the active pass progress, polar trajectory and transceivers info
Showing the satellite positional data, footprint and ground track on the map
Custom TLE satellite data import
Offline calculations with recommended weekly TLE data updates
primary_use_case: Tracking and predicting satellite passes on an Android device.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, friendly robot astronaut holding a glowing holographic map of Earth. The map displays intricate orbital paths of satellites, with specific paths highlighted and glowing, indicating upcoming passes. Small, transparent screens float around the robot, showing simplified trajectory lines and countdown timers. The robot is pointing towards a specific satellite path on the map with a gentle, guiding gesture. The background is a deep space vista with subtle star patterns and the 'Look4Sat' name subtly integrated into a constellation. The overall mood is helpful and informative, using a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with a vibrant blue and white color palette.

name: AndroidForensics
description: Automated Android forensic toolkit: extract device info, apps, contacts, call logs, SMS, system logs, and more via ADB for security audits and investigations.
readme_summary: AndroidForensics is a toolkit and guide that leverages ADB commands to extract various data artifacts from Android devices for security audits and investigations. It focuses on gathering system information, installed applications, logs, network data, and user activity without requiring root access on non-rooted devices.
key_features: Extract device system information (model, OS version, serial number)
List and export installed applications
Collect system logs (logcat)
Retrieve battery and power data
Dump network configuration and connection details
Pull specific directories and files (e.g., DCIM, Downloads)
Gather device timeline and activity data (usagestats, batterystats)
Extract accounts, contacts, call logs, and messages (with caveats for newer Android versions)
primary_use_case: Automated extraction of digital artifacts from Android devices via ADB for forensic analysis, security audits, and investigations.
cover_image_prompt: A digital detective, depicted as a stylized magnifying glass with a circuit board pattern, meticulously examining a holographic Android phone. The phone displays glowing icons representing contacts, call logs, SMS messages, and system logs, all being carefully extracted and organized into a secure digital vault. Subtle lines of code and data streams flow from the phone to the vault, illuminated by the focused beam of the magnifying glass. The background is a dark, abstract digital landscape with faint network lines. The overall mood is one of careful investigation and data retrieval. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a blue/green color palette.

name: taste-skill
description: Taste-Skill - gives your AI good taste. stops the AI from generating boring, generic slop
readme_summary: Taste Skill is a framework for AI agents that enhances the quality of AI-generated frontend interfaces. It provides portable 'Agent Skills' to improve layout, typography, motion, and spacing, moving beyond generic UI designs. It also includes image-generation skills for creating reference boards and design assets.
key_features: Portable Agent Skills for AI interfaces
Improves layout, typography, motion, and spacing
Generates image assets for reference boards (web, mobile, brand kits)
Compatible with tools like ChatGPT Images, Codex, Cursor, and Claude Code
Installs via `npx skills add` CLI
primary_use_case: To elevate the quality and aesthetic appeal of AI-generated frontend user interfaces by providing specialized skills that go beyond generic design.
cover_image_prompt: A master sculptor, with a discerning eye, carefully chiseling a rough block of marble into a sleek, modern, and elegant user interface. The block of marble represents raw AI output, with generic, unrefined shapes. The sculptor's tools are glowing with digital energy, subtly hinting at AI and code. As the sculptor works, the UI takes shape with perfect typography, balanced spacing, and subtle motion lines emanating from the design. In the background, a digital canvas displays a mood board of premium frontend designs. The overall scene is clean, sophisticated, and bathed in soft, inspiring light, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on premium aesthetics and intuitive design.
